title: Melodorum scortechinii
taxonomy: Melodorum scortechinii is a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae. It belongs to the genus Melodorum and is a type of evergreen tree native to Southeast Asia. The classification of Melodorum scortechinii places it within the order Magnoliales, which is a group of flowering plants known for their distinctive flowers and seeds. The family Annonaceae is a large and diverse group of plants, often referred to as the custard apple family. Melodorum scortechinii's classification reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within this family.

The plant's roots and rhizomes are often used to create remedies for fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory diseases. In some parts of Asia, Melodorum scortechinii is also used to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and dermatitis.
